full_text:
**[Dear Dr. Michele Hartsough,]{.mark}**

[We are pleased to submit our manuscript, titled
**"**]{.mark}**Single-cell spatial atlas of high-grade serous ovarian
cancer uncovers MHC class II as a key predictor of spatial tumor
ecosystems and clinical outcomes[,"]{.mark}** [for consideration in
*Cancer Discovery*. This work presents a clinically and molecularly
resolved single-cell spatial atlas of 280 metastatic ovarian cancers,
integrating spatial proteomics, genomics, bulk, single-cell, and spatial
transcriptomics to map the tumor-immune microenvironment (TME) at
unprecedented scale and resolution. **As discussed in the email (25th
June 2025), we have now included supporting functional data from our
patient-derived organoid platform, and we would be grateful for your
feedback on the manuscript. **]{.mark}

[High-grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC), the most lethal gynecologic
malignancy, has shown limited responses to immunotherapy. Prior work by
our group and others has demonstrated that tumor-immune interactions
drive responses to chemotherapy (*Launonen 2021 Nat Commun*; *Launonen
2024 Cancer Cell*) and immunotherapy combinations (*Färkkilä 2019 Nat
Commun*). However, improving therapeutic strategies and patient
stratification requires a comprehensive understanding of how the tumor
ecosystem is orchestrated in HGSC.]{.mark}

Commun*). However, improving therapeutic strategies and patient
stratification requires a comprehensive understanding of how the tumor
ecosystem is orchestrated in HGSC.]{.mark}

[In this study, we present a multi-omics, spatially resolved analysis of
the TME in metastatic HGSC with a total number of 15.1 million cells
across multiomic datasets. Using 4.6 million single cells across 929
high-dimensional maps, we identify distinct spatial domains that
correspond to clinicomolecular subtypes and are predictive of clinical
outcomes. To uncover key features of these tumor ecosystems, we
developed CEFIIRA (Cell Feature Importance Identification by Random
Forest)---a versatile, open-source machine-learning pipeline. This tool
robustly identifies clinically relevant features, such as
tumor-intrinsic MHC-II expression, which we found to be a critical
driver of prolonged survival. Tumor-intrinsic MHC-II also mediates
immune infiltration, antigen presentation, and responses to
immunotherapy *in vitro*, providing novel insights into the immune
surveillance mechanisms across HGSC subtypes.]{.mark}

**[Key Novelties and Impact of Our Work:]{.mark}**

1. **[Unprecedented Scale and Resolution of the HGSC Single-cell
 > Spatial Atlas:]{.mark}**

 - [We present a uniquely large, clinically annotated, multi-omics
 > dataset of 280 HGSC tumors, integrating genomics,

lution of the HGSC Single-cell
 > Spatial Atlas:]{.mark}**

 - [We present a uniquely large, clinically annotated, multi-omics
 > dataset of 280 HGSC tumors, integrating genomics,
 > transcriptomics, and spatial imaging with comprehensive
 > clinical data.]{.mark}

 - [Publicly available, clinically and molecularly annotated
 > single-cell spatial dataset. We will make the annotated
 > single-cell spatial data with molecular profiles, therapy
 > sequences, and clinical survival data publicly available. This
 > will be an extremely valuable resource to the research
 > community for additional hypothesis generation and
 > exploration.]{.mark}

 - [Our manuscript provides an unparalleled resource and conceptual
 > framework for understanding tumor-immune interactions in HGSC.
 > We believe that our findings will significantly advance
 > efforts in biomarker discovery, therapeutic development, and
 > patient stratification, with broad implications for cancer
 > research.]{.mark}

2. **[Development of the CEFIIRA Machine-Learning Pipeline:]{.mark}**

 - [**CEFIIRA** is a robust and generalizable machine-learning tool
 > that identifies spatial and molecular features critical for
 > tumor-immune interactions. It incorporates cross-validation,
 > feature elimination, and feature permutation across iterative
 > training and testing cycles to minimize bias and
 > overfitting.]{.mark}

ns. It incorporates cross-validation,
 > feature elimination, and feature permutation across iterative
 > training and testing cycles to minimize bias and
 > overfitting.]{.mark}

 - [As open-source software, CEFIIRA enables the integration of
 > multi-level datasets, creating a scalable tool for cancer
 > research and other diseases.]{.mark}

3. [**Improved Understanding and Clinical Relevance of TME spatial
 > structures in HGSC.**]{.mark}

 - [In addition to tumor compositions and immune cell infiltration,
 > we present a systematic analysis of the tumor spatial domains
 > in HGSC.]{.mark}

 - [We show that, particularly, the tumor-stroma interface
 > composition is associated with distinct clinical
 > outcomes.]{.mark}

 - [Our work also further highlights the impact of the tumor
 > molecular profiles and chemotherapy exposure on the spatial
 > and functional TME. Thus, our systematic assessment of our
 > multiomics dataset brings important considerations into future
 > TME analyses in HGSC.]{.mark}

4. **[Discovery of Tumor-Intrinsic MHC-II as a Critical
 > Biomarker:]{.mark}**

 - [We demonstrate that spatially heterogeneous, tumor-intrinsic
 > MHC-II expression associates with clinical outcomes in HGSC,
 > independently of the clinicomolecular subtypes.]{.mark}

 - [Using our two distinct spatial transcriptomics datasets, we

I expression associates with clinical outcomes in HGSC,
 > independently of the clinicomolecular subtypes.]{.mark}

 - [Using our two distinct spatial transcriptomics datasets, we
 > show that tumor-intrinsic MHCII expression is associated with
 > local immune infiltration and activation.]{.mark}

 - [Using mass spectrometry immunopeptidomics, we show that MHCII
 > binds to specific immunopeptides in clinical tumors. This,
 > together with increased TCR clonality associated with
 > tumor-intrinsic MHCII, highlights a role for the local tumor
 > microenvironment niches in the overall immunogenicity in
 > HGSC.]{.mark}

 - **Using a functional patient-derived immuno-oncology platform,
 > we show that tumor MHCII expression associates with enhanced
 > cytotoxicity of CD8+ T cells after immune checkpoint
 > inhibition.**

[Altogether, our findings have significant clinical implications,
suggesting that MHC-II expression predicts overall tumor immunogenicity,
and guide the development of alternative therapeutic approaches for
MHC-II--negative HGSCs.]{.mark}

[Thank you for considering our manuscript for publication. We look
forward to the opportunity to contribute to *Cancer Discovery*.]{.mark}

[Yours sincerely,]{.mark}
